meaning: adj.产生幻觉的,幻影的,错觉的,虚假的,不牢靠的
examples: "It's an illusory sense of being trapped," said Robinson.
The idea that there is a religious basis for this clash is illusory, for not even the Pope has been able to control it.
Day trading, which made no sense even in the best of times, is now 100% illusory.
It also really happened, to real people, not illusory figures like John Wayne or Errol Flynn.
